---
title: "Use colorblind-friendly palette for visualization"
date: 2022-01-22
image: colorblind_palette.jpg
description: "Use friendly colors in a graph with ggplot2"
categories:
  - R
  - colors
---





![](colorblind_palette.jpg)

## Gray colors 


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
### gray colors
cb <- c("gray", "lightgray", "darkgray", "black")
barplot(1:4, col = cb)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-1-1.png){fig-align='center' width=85%}
:::
:::

## Friendly colors


::: {.cell layout-ncol="2" layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
## Use color blind-friendly palette:
cbp1 <- c(
  "#999999", 
  "#E69F00", 
  "#56B4E9", 
  "#009E73",
  "#F0E442", 
  "#0072B2", 
  "#D55E00", 
  "#CC79A7"
)
barplot(1:8, col = cbp1)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-2-1.png){fig-align='center' width=85%}
:::
:::

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
### friendly colors
cbPalette <- c(
    "#000000", 
    "#999999", 
    "#E69F00", 
    "#56B4E9", 
    "#009E73", 
    "#F0E442", 
    "#0072B2", 
    "#D55E00", 
    "#CC79A7"
)
barplot(1:8, col = cbPalette)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){fig-align='center' width=85%}
:::
:::

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(ggplot2)
friendly_colors <- c(
  "#4C72B0", 
  "#55A868", 
  "#C44E52", 
  "#8172B2", 
  "#CCB974", 
  "#64B5CD"
)

# Create example data frame
df <- data.frame(
    group = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F"),
    value = c(10, 20, 15, 25, 30, 35)
)

# Create plot with friendly colors
ggplot(df, aes(x = group, y = value, fill = group)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = friendly_colors)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-1.png){fig-align='center' width=85%}
:::
:::
